strategy_summary: VIX at 21.92 (+10% spike), SPY -0.79%, broad risk-off. Strategy shifted to defensive rotation: prioritize consumer staples (XLP), healthcare (XLV held via previous entry), utilities (XLU mean reversion). FXY crashed -28% and was closed at hard stop. No new momentum plays until VIX recedes below 18.

daily_summary.action: Closed FXY (-28% hard stop), entered XLP defensive rotation
daily_summary.note: VIX spike to 21.92 (+10%) confirms risk-off regime. FXY mean reversion thesis failed catastrophically — yen continued weakening. Lesson: FXY/NOK/FXE currency ETFs have asymmetric risk in trending FX markets. MR on currencies requires tighter stops. Portfolio pivoting to pure defensives (XLP, XLU, XLV) until VIX normalizes.
daily_summary.lesson_learned: Currency ETF mean reversion is higher risk than equity MR — FX trends persist longer. FXY position didn't have a tight enough stop at entry. Moving forward: currency ETFs get -7% hard stops, not -5% trailing.
